title: api: input structs for multi-parameter methods (no more too_many_arguments)
description:
message_create has 8 positional &str args (#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]). message_query has 7. As filters grow (text, threadRootId, after/before, has_attachment), this gets worse across all 30+ methods.

Design: define typed input structs with Default for every method that takes more than ~4 parameters:

  #[derive(Default)]
  pub struct MessageCreate<'a> {
      pub chat_id: &'a Id,
      pub body: &'a str,
      pub body_type: &'a str,
      pub sent_at: &'a UTCDate,
      pub reply_to: Option<&'a Id>,
      pub client_id: Option<&'a str>,
  }

  #[derive(Default)]
  pub struct MessageQuery {
      pub chat_id: Option<Id>,
      pub has_mention: Option<bool>,
      pub has_attachment: Option<bool>,
      pub text: Option<String>,
      pub thread_root_id: Option<Id>,
      pub position: Option<u64>,
      pub limit: Option<u64>,
  }

Define structs for: MessageCreate, MessageQuery, ChatQuery, ChatSet, and each new method added in method epics. Place in a methods/args.rs module.

Remove all #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] after migration.

Tests: each struct must impl Default and construct correctly with partial initialization.
